1.Title: RPS Drop Despite Steady Users
1.Insight: While virtual users for Homepage remained constant at **10**, [response time](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated significantly and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) dropped to **2.0 RPS** by 9:20 PM UTC. This correlated slowdown suggests the server experienced increasing latency, likely due to resource contention or backend limitations.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) were stable at **10** from 9:18 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC, dropping to **7** at 9:20 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) started at 3.6 RPS at 9:18 PM UTC, peaked at 6.4 RPS by 9:19 PM UTC, and declined to 2.0 RPS at 9:20 PM UTC despite stable user counts. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated between 1840ms and 2430ms from 9:19 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC, with a trough at **1840ms** at 9:20 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider investigating server-side bottlenecks—such as database, network, or compute resource limitations. Profile application performance at higher RPS levels and review backend scaling configurations.

2.Title: Response Time Fluctuation Under Load
2.Insight: Observed substantial [response time](#ClientMetrics) variability for Homepage with peaks up to **2430ms** and troughs as low as **1840ms**, while virtual users remained stable. This suggests inconsistent server responsiveness under load.
2.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 3240ms at 9:18 PM UTC to 2090ms at 9:19 PM UTC, then fluctuated between 1840ms and 2430ms from 9:19 PM UTC to 9:20 PM UTC. Notable peaks were observed at 9:19 PM UTC (**2400ms**), 9:20 PM UTC (**2250ms**), and 9:20 PM UTC (**2430ms**). [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) varied but remained between 5.0 and 6.1 RPS during these response time fluctuations.
2.Recommendation: Consider enabling server-side monitoring and logging during load tests to pinpoint bottlenecks. Review application code for inefficient operations that may cause periodic delays under concurrent access.

3.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Response times fluctuated with multiple peaks, while throughput dropped toward the end, indicating potential backend constraints.
